ops.title: Fleet AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md (and some install-binary.sh) are cc-guides-rendered from .claude/fragments/<target>/ — edit fragments + render + commit both together; artifact-only edits self-revert

ops.body:
As of 2026-07-10 (v3 layout composition, superseding the v1 `CLAUDE.src.md` era), all 21 fleet repos render their managed artifacts — `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and for slop-cop/cc-review/cc-runtime an `install-binary.sh` — from `.claude/fragments/<target-path>/` (local `*.fragment.md|sh` + `layout.toml`, shared prose imported as `cc-skills:<name>` from cc-skills `guides/`). The rendered file carries a `cc-guides <ver> src=… fragments=… | GENERATED` banner; `.github/workflows/guides.yml` checks freshness and re-renders on dispatch/schedule.

**Why:** an edit to the rendered artifact alone passes locally and pushes fine, then the next re-render composes from the unchanged fragments and lands a `chore: re-render guides` commit that silently reverts the edit on main@origin (observed live in the v1 era: a 31-repo re-stamp "succeeded" 29/31, only 14 survived).

**How to apply:** edit the fragments under `.claude/fragments/<target>/` (never the artifact), run `cc-guides render`, and commit fragments + artifact **in one commit**; verify against `main@origin` after the Guides CI settles, not at push time. layout.toml must be fragments-FIRST, `[sources.*]` last (TOML nesting footgun; ErrFragmentsNested). Shared-prose changes go in cc-skills `guides/` instead — the fan-out re-renders every importer. cc-context plugin ≥0.9.0 enforces this: PreToolUse BLOCKS direct edits of banner-stamped artifacts whose layout.toml exists, and nudges once per session on fragment edits. Full architecture: cc-notes note `2c555db` in cc-context ([[cc-guides-v3-architecture]]). Related: [[model-routing-convention]], [[shared-working-copy-concurrency]].
